mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into positive short-term equity momentum as firms report higher operational efficiency.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and elevated consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are elevated, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects aggregate economic expansion that directly supports corporate profit growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the correlation between GDPC1 changes and equity returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative forward returns in >65% of triggered months, or payroll growth above 150k consistently coincides with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stagnant retail sales indicates unwanted inventory accumulation, forcing corporate margin compression and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive when industrial production outpaces retail sales, or the inventory buildup signal fails to predict drawdowns in >55% of cases.
